category_title: Students who reject SNA support to seek peer-like autonomy and social inclusion
scenario_title: Paired-work activities with SNA as partner
minimal_context: The student is assigned to complete a paired-work activity with the Special Needs Assistant acting as their partner.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: It is time for the paired-work activity. I will be your partner today.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I want to be like everyone else in the class.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's just get started on the first question together.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Sits back and pushes the worksheet toward the tutor) You do it.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think the answer is?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don't want anyone sitting next to me. I want to be like the boys.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: It is time for the paired-work activity. I will be your partner today.

STUDENT: I want to be like everyone else in the class.

TUTOR: Let's just get started on the first question together.

STUDENT: (Sits back and pushes the worksheet toward the tutor) You do it.

TUTOR: What do you think the answer is?

STUDENT: I don't want anyone sitting next to me. I want to be like the boys.

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When partnered with SNAs in lieu of classmates
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Concerns of target pupils becoming socially and emotionally dependent on SNAs
related_quotes: He doesn’t want to be dependent, he wants to be like the boys

behavior_patterns.condition: When assigned an SNA as a partner instead of a peer
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Outlined over-reliance on SNAs during paired-work
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Wants to be like everyone else in the class
related_quotes: He doesn’t want to be dependent, he wants to be like the boys
